rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DONALD EDWARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/2021. Responsive papers filed late on 3/25 for a 3/29 hearing, cannot be viewed by examiner in the court system, and examiner has not received the courtesy copies (5) court days prior to the hearing. In order for supplemental or opposition papers to be considered by the Court prior to the hearing, or for supplements, amendments, or fully amended pleadings addressing issues raised by an Examiner to be reviewed prior to a continued hearing, unless otherwise directed by the Court, papers must be filed at least seven (7) court days before the hearing, and the filing party must deliver courtesy copies to the Examiners or Investigators no later than five (5) court days before the hearing with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet and any cover letter. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ENID J. THOMAS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #12, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/10/21. (No earlier availability.) Bond must be increased before an account can be approved. [CRC 7.204.] The calculation of the amount of bond in paragraph 7 is slightly off. The attorney fee total on page 4/6 of the time sheets is $5,777.50, not $5,817.50. (The total fees and costs in the prayer, however, appears to be correct.) The filing fee for this petition was $450, not $465. Attorney costs are usually paid out of income, not one-half out of principal. Does the trust direct all fees and costs to be paid one-half out of principal and one-half out of income? The attorney stated at the hearing on 6/12/19 that someone in the office of the successor trustee is doing the accounting, but this account shows that Martin Harris was paid $926.25 to prepare the 11th account. Additionally, $14,850.35 was paid to First Republic for advisory fees. After procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will be required for the Court to determine if the fees requested by the successor trustee are reasonable. (It would be helpful in future petitions if the terms of the testamentary trust were incorporated into the accounting petition, so that the Court does not have to track down the order creating it in a prior case in order to confirm who the beneficiaries are and whether they are current, remainder, or contingent.) A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/29/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD EDWARD STEVENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/26/2021. 1) Examiner still has not received the required courtesy copy of the petition. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. 3) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID M. LERMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/21. Paragraph 5 of the petition indicates that all expenses of administration have been paid, but the disbursements schedule does not reflect a payment to the Probate Referee. The calculation of the statutory fee in paragraph 25 of the petition is incorrect: receipts (less principal receipts such as refunds) are missing, and it appears that the losses on sales was added rather than subtracted. NB: An amendment to a petition requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. If an amendment to the petition cannot be filed in time to give notice of the hearing on 5/3/21, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request additional time. A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/22/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARION JENKINS AND GANAIL JENKINS LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Instructing Trustee To Place Property In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/21. (No earlier availability.) The successor trustee resides in Oakland. How is San Francisco the proper venue? The correct form of notice is Judicial Council form DE-115. If this is the proper venue, this Court requires copies of the bank statements immediately preceding the date of death to confirm how title to the accounts was vested. Probate Code Section 851 requires notice with a copy of the petition to a trust beneficiary, but an heir who is not a trust beneficiary, who would stand to inherit if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/5/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELEANOR MCCLATCHY TRUST FOR THE PRIMARY BENEFIT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Co-Trustees For Settlement And Approval Of Trustees' Fourth Account, And The Trustees' Acts Thereunder
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/2021. 1) Please explain where the principal place of administration of the trust is today. 2) As this is reportedly the fourth account but only the first before the Court, the co-trustees must provide legal authority and verify the values of the assets on hand at the beginning of this accounting period. Without such verification, the Court could only find and order that the account balances. 3) The list of trust beneficiaries also does not indicate under which remainder subtrust for a nephew listed in Article II of the trust agreement the named individuals would be beneficiaries. 4) There does not appear to be an order appointing a guardian ad litem for the minor remainder beneficiary. His age is also not set forth. Notice must be given directly to a minor who is at least 12 years old and also the person having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d) and Probate Code Section 1460.1.] 5) This and the two related trusts are reportedly to be combined and distributed, but the Stipulation Staying All Proceedings Pending Appeal, etc., filed on 3/10/21 in case PTR-11-294985 (the "James Trust"), at paragraph 1 seems to refer to just that "Action" and not to this action. 6) Please explain and provide legal authority in support of the alleged Liabilities. 7) Please explain the end balance in the account of Bank of America checking x9588 ending at $2,239.60 when the bank statement appears to show $2,178.20 assets on 12/31/2017. Appears the account may need to be amended. 8) Please explain Charles McClatchy is listed in the petition on page 8 with different addresses and it appears both Charles McClatchy's in the trust have not received direct notice. The trust appears to show Charles K. McClatchy and a son as Charles McClatchy. Please indicate whether Charles K. McClatchy or his son Charles McClatchy are deceased and provide direct notice to both individuals if both are still alive. (Probate Code 1202). A petition and account involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. (CRC 7.902). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN L. BERGQUIST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/2021. 1) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/5/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
